evals.prompt: Review the checkout flow UI on my branch — 4 changed components, dev server runs on 3000.
evals.expected_output: Structured verdict with coverage statement, findings grouped by action label, evidence per finding. No source files modified.
assertions: Drives the browser once and produces a shared capture bundle
Does NOT let each lens drive the browser independently
Emits Result, Scope, Design source, Coverage, Lenses run in the header
Opens with what works well before the findings
Every finding prefixed with [blocking], [warning], or [suggestion]
Every visual finding cites a screenshot path plus state and viewport
Modifies no file outside .ux-review/ and .agency/reviews/
Closes by pointing at ux-design-review-fix rather than offering to fix

evals.prompt: Review the UI. The page has a live 'time remaining' counter and a rotating hero carousel.
evals.expected_output: Dynamic regions stabilised or masked; differences across paired captures reported as flake, not deviations.
assertions: Disables animation and waits for fonts before capturing
Captures twice and compares
Marks the counter and carousel as unstable regions in the manifest
Does NOT report the varying regions as design-fidelity deviations
Caps confidence at Tentative for any finding resting on an unstable region

evals.prompt: Check accessibility on the new payment form. Axe reports zero violations.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to claim conformance from the scan; runs the manual criteria from the traversal record.
assertions: Does NOT report 'accessibility passed' on the basis of a clean scan
States that zero violations means the automatable portion passed and nothing more
Judges focus order (2.4.3) and focus visibility (2.4.7) from the keyboard traversal record
Judges reflow (1.4.10) from the 320-equivalent capture
Names which manual criteria were verified in the coverage statement
Marks manual criteria unverified rather than passed when the traversal record is missing

evals.prompt: Review the summary card. It has 12px padding, the Figma shows 16px, and the repo has a space-400 token set to 16px.
evals.expected_output: One merged finding carrying both the screenshot and the file:line, with the token named as the fix.
assertions: Reports this as ONE finding, not separate fidelity and design-system findings
Cites both a screenshot and a file:line as evidence
Names space-400 as the remediation rather than prescribing 16px
Takes Design Fidelity as the category with the system cause recorded

evals.prompt: Review the UI. The error message overflows its container at 375px but looks fine at 1440px.
evals.expected_output: Caught by the experience lens at the state-by-viewport intersection.
assertions: Exercises the error state at the small viewport, not only the default state
Reports the finding once with the viewport recorded, not once per viewport
States the user impact rather than prescribing a pixel fix

evals.prompt: Review the UI. No dev server starts — the app needs backend env vars I don't have.
evals.expected_output: Degrades to static-only, states reduced coverage, does not fake live results.
assertions: Does not spend long fighting the environment before degrading
Runs the conformance system pass and markup-level accessibility subset
Marks all live-only checks unverified rather than passed
States the reduced coverage explicitly in the verdict
A PASS is explicitly partial

evals.prompt: Re-review the checkout UI. I fixed the focus ring, and the designer updated the Figma since your last pass.
evals.expected_output: Incremental review; design-side drift detected and reported separately from code changes.
assertions: Reads .agency/reviews/ux-{branch}.json and reviews incrementally
Compares the design source ref and detects it changed
Re-derives fidelity findings for components whose design moved even where code did not change
Reports 'newly diverged because the design moved' separately from newly introduced
Does not re-raise accepted deviations or dismissed findings

evals.prompt: Review this UI change. The mockup specifies a placeholder colour that fails 4.5:1 contrast.
evals.expected_output: Accessibility wins; the design source is flagged as the problem.
assertions: Raises the contrast failure as a blocking Accessibility finding
Does NOT treat matching the mockup as satisfying the review
Flags the design source itself as needing correction
Applies the accessibility override regardless of severity band
